issues.title: BM25F weights fallback condition changed from <= 0 to < 0
issues.description: The condition for falling back to default BM25F weights changed from `<= 0` to `< 0`. This means weights of exactly 0 no longer trigger the fallback to defaults (Title=2.0, Snippet=1.0). Instead, ComputeBM25FScores clamps them to 1.0. If a user explicitly sets BM25FWeights to 0 in their config, the old behavior would use defaults, but the new behavior uses the clamped values.
issues.suggestion: If zero values should trigger defaults, keep `<= 0`. If zero should be treated as explicit values that get clamped, the new behavior is intentional but the semantics changed.
issues.impact: Configuration files setting BM25F weights to 0 would previously get defaults (2.0, 1.0) but now get clamped values (1.0, 1.0), changing search ranking behavior.

issues.title: Removed panic recovery allows goroutine panics to crash the process
issues.description: The `defer func() { _ = recover() }()` was removed from the scrape goroutine in `scrapeAll`. This means any panic originating from `scraper.Scrape` or subsequent data access will propagate up and crash the entire process instead of being silently caught.
issues.suggestion: Restore the recover or ensure scraper operations are sandboxed. If panics are expected from the scraper when handling untrusted URLs, restore the recover. If panics should crash the process, add a comment explaining this is intentional.
issues.impact: When scraping untrusted URLs or when the scraper encounters an internal bug, the process will crash instead of gracefully skipping the failed result. Users will see process termination instead of a partial response.

issues.title: Shell command injection via unescaped URL in generateCurlCommand
issues.description: The generateCurlCommand function embeds baseUrl directly into a shell command string without escaping shell metacharacters. While the new code escapes single quotes in the JSON body, the URL itself is placed in single quotes without any sanitization. An attacker who controls baseUrl can inject shell commands.
issues.suggestion: Escape the URL using encodeURIComponent or implement proper shell quoting for the fullUrl variable before embedding in the curl command.
issues.impact: Remote code execution on user machines when they copy and execute the generated curl command from a compromised playground deployment.

issues.title: Health check response caching may expose stale status for security-sensitive operations
issues.description: The new checkHealthCached function caches HealthResponse for 5000ms without any validation or cache invalidation mechanism. If health checks are used for availability monitoring or authentication gates, returning cached responses can mask critical failures.
issues.suggestion: Remove caching for health checks or implement proper cache invalidation when errors occur.
issues.impact: Stale health status may mask server failures, potentially causing requests to be routed to unhealthy instances.

issues.title: Removed panic recovery in scrapeAll exposes goroutine panics to process termination
issues.description: The diff removes `defer func() { _ = recover() }()` that was catching panics in the scraping goroutines. Now, if `scraper.Scrape` panics, the entire Go process terminates instead of gracefully handling the error.
issues.suggestion: Restore the panic recovery in the goroutine: 'defer func() { _ = recover() }()' before the semaphore acquisition.
issues.impact: Process termination (Denial of Service) when any scraped URL triggers a panic in the scraper, affecting all concurrent requests.

issues.title: Health cache grows unbounded without eviction
issues.description: The healthCache Map has no eviction mechanism for stale entries. When an entry expires (TTL check fails), the code adds a new entry via healthCache.set() but the old expired entry remains in the Map forever. If checkHealthCached is called with many distinct URLs, the Map grows unboundedly.
issues.suggestion: Delete the existing entry before setting a new one, or implement a TTL cache that auto-evicts stale entries.
issues.impact: Memory grows linearly with unique URLs queried. Long-running server processes will accumulate unbounded Map entries.
